sources.notes: Hard version of chain prefix rank on a tree.
Official tutorial optimizes the easy tree DP with data structures and binary search over ranks.
Luogu solution gives additional details for the hard-version optimization.

annotation.summary: Compute chain prefix ranks on a tree under hard constraints.
annotation.constraints: n is large; the quadratic DP from F1 must be optimized with data structures.
annotation.core_idea: Keep the rooted-tree DP interpretation from F1, but avoid materializing all rank tables. Rank thresholds are monotone, so binary search can locate the boundary where a chain prefix contributes. Fenwick/segment-tree style structures over DFS order store active child contributions while processing each node, allowing subtree contributions to be queried and merged logarithmically.
annotation.complexity: O(n log^2 n) or O(n log n) depending on implementation.
tricks: optimize tree DP with DFS-order data structure
merge child contributions without quadratic tables

solution_variants.name: dfs-order-rank-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use DFS-order range structures and binary search on rank thresholds to compress the easy DP transitions.

tags.evidence: The hard version still solves a tree DP over chain-prefix contributions.
DFS-order range aggregates can be maintained with Fenwick-like structures.
The rank contribution boundary is found by binary search.
All states and queries are over rooted-tree chains/subtrees.
